fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

top_risks: [MEDIUM] Fusion debug signals: mismatches and missing fused values
summary.notes: Diagnostics are heuristic and dataset-agnostic; confirm by inspecting linked artifacts for representative samples.

findings.title: Fusion debug signals: mismatches and missing fused values
findings.description: Debug reasons indicate where fusion diverges from gold. A high mismatch share suggests normalization/selection issues; missing fused values suggest rule coverage gaps or dropped attributes.
findings.recommendation: Sample the top mismatch cases; add attribute normalizers and strengthen value selection logic; ensure fusion keeps candidate values for sparse attributes.

findings.title: Input source size imbalance
findings.description: One or more sources are much smaller than others, which can reduce match coverage or bias fusion toward larger sources.
findings.recommendation: Verify that all sources were fully ingested and filtered consistently; check preprocessing steps that may drop many records from a source.

recommendations: Sample the top mismatch cases; add attribute normalizers and strengthen value selection logic; ensure fusion keeps candidate values for sparse attributes.
Verify that all sources were fully ingested and filtered consistently; check preprocessing steps that may drop many records from a source.
Add automated checks for largest clusters (top-N) and fail the pipeline when max cluster size or one-to-many ratio exceeds a threshold.
Track per-attribute normalization coverage (parse success rates for dates/durations) and log before/after canonical values for mismatches.
Persist and version matching thresholds/blocking configuration alongside outputs to make drift explainable and reversible.

[source]

Bases:
BasePostClusterer
Finds optimal one-to-one matching using maximum bipartite matching.
This algorithm formulates the entity matching problem as a maximum weight
bipartite matching problem and finds the globally optimal solution using
the Hungarian algorithm or similar optimization methods.
Unlike the greedy approach, this guarantees the optimal solution that
maximizes the total similarity score while enforcing one-to-one constraints.
Requires NetworkX for the bipartite matching implementation.
Examples
>>>
matcher
=
MaximumBipartiteMatching
()
>>>

(

):
"""Finds optimal one-to-one matching using maximum bipartite matching.
This algorithm formulates the entity matching problem as a maximum weight
bipartite matching problem and finds the globally optimal solution using
the Hungarian algorithm or similar optimization methods.
Unlike the greedy approach, this guarantees the optimal solution that
maximizes the total similarity score while enforcing one-to-one constraints.

Module contents

Provenance-aware I/O helpers for PyDI.
This package exposes convenience wrappers around pandas readers that attach
provenance metadata and add a unique id column.

-----

normalize_quantity
Parse and optionally convert units + expand scale modifiers
normalize_column
Normalize a pandas Series of quantities
Scale Modifiers

detect_scale_modifier
Detect MEO, MEUR, million, thousand, etc. in text
expand_scale
Expand “5 MEO” → 5,000,000
parse_scaled_number
Parse number with scale modifier
Integrations (External Libraries)

normalize_country, normalize_currency, normalize_language
ISO code normalization via pycountry
validate_stdnum, format_stdnum, normalize_stdnum
Standard number handling via python-stdnum
parse_phone, format_phone, validate_phone
Phone number handling via phonenumbers
validate_email, normalize_email
Email handling via email-validator
Specification & Transformation (New API)

NormalizationSpec
Define how columns should be normalized
ColumnSpec
Per-column normalization settings
transform_dataframe
Apply transformations according to spec
normalize_dataframe
Main entry point with optional auto-detection
Usage Examples

Profile a DataFrame:
>>> from PyDI.normalization import profile_dataframe
>>> profile = profile_dataframe(df)
>>> print(profile.summary())
Normalize quantities:
>>> from PyDI.normalization import normalize_quantity
